Title: The Rhus chinensis Genome Provides Insights Into Tannin, Flavonoid Biosynthesis, and Glandular Trichome Development

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by salt stress; highly represented within expanded chitinase family expressed in fruit/root/flower contexts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly and strongly induced under 400 mM NaCl; selected from expanded chitinase family responsive to heat/salt
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (400 mM NaCl); heat stress (45°C in leaf discs, 53°C in yeast)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient overexpression in R. chinensis leaf discs; yeast heterologous expression survival assay; physiological ROS and antioxidant enzyme assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances salt and heat tolerance and reduces ROS accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcChiC is an expanded chitinase-family member induced by salt stress. 2. Transient overexpression in R. chinensis leaf discs improved tolerance to 400 mM NaCl and 45°C heat. 3. RcChiC increased CAT/POD activities and reduced H2O2 and O2•− accumulation; yeast expressing RcChiC showed higher survival under salt and heat.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core study focus is stress resilience rather than a specific nutrient metabolite product; included as a core experimentally validated common gene in Results.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for engineering salinity/heat tolerance in R. chinensis or other crops.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reached >25-fold transcript increase after 24 h salt exposure
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (400 mM NaCl); heat stress (45°C in leaf discs, 53°C in yeast)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient overexpression in R. chinensis leaf discs; yeast heterologous expression survival assay; physiological ROS and antioxidant enzyme assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances salt and heat tolerance and lowers oxidative damage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcCTL1 is a salt-inducible expanded chitinase-family gene. 2. Overexpression in R. chinensis leaf discs improved heat and salinity tolerance. 3. RcCTL1 elevated CAT/POD activities and reduced ROS; yeast expressing RcCTL1 had higher survival under stress.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated as part of expanded defense-associated chitinase family contributing to stress adaptation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for breeding or engineering abiotic stress tolerance.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step supplying cinnamate for general phenylpropanoid and downstream flavonoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root, flower, and fruit; upregulated under salt and drought stress, downregulated under heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress; drought stress; heat stress; transient overexpression assays under 45°C heat and 300 mM NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; transient overexpression in tobacco leaves; flavonoid quantification; yeast survival assay; stress physiology assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes total flavonoid accumulation and improves salt/heat tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcPAL belongs to an expanded PAL family in R. chinensis driven by tandem duplication. 2. RcPAL is highly expressed in flavonoid-rich tissues and stress-responsive. 3. Transient overexpression in tobacco significantly increased total flavonoid content and enhanced tolerance to heat and salt.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity; plant defense against abiotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to tested gene as RcPAL; accession corresponds to one highly expressed PAL copy.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising for increasing flavonoid content together with stress resilience in medicinal or crop plants.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSA: CRA012509; CRA012506; CRA012504; CRA012500); metabolomics of flavonoid content

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms central precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root, flower, and fruit; certain CHS genes induced under salt and drought stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress; drought stress; heat stress; transient overexpression assays under 45°C heat and 300 mM NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; transient overexpression in tobacco leaves; flavonoid quantification; yeast survival assay; stress physiology assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes total flavonoid accumulation and improves salt/heat tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcCHS is from a strongly expanded CHS family generated by tandem duplication. 2. RcCHS is highly expressed in flavonoid-rich tissues and stress-responsive. 3. Transient overexpression in tobacco increased flavonoid content and improved tolerance to salt and heat.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tested representative CHS gene is one of the tandem-expanded copies in R. chinensis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme target to elevate flavonoids and stress tolerance in crops or medicinal plants.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSA: CRA012509; CRA012506; CRA012504; CRA012500); metabolomics of flavonoid content

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydrolyzable tannin hydrolysis and gallic acid release
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream hydrolysis step linked to gallic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes galloylated tannin substrates such as EGCG and PGG to release gallic acid, and EGC from EGCG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as central/key enzyme for gallic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers, developing fruit, and especially Galla chinensis relative to leaves; induced by salt and drought
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently overexpressed in tobacco leaves; highly expressed in Galla chinensis tissues of R. chinensis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress; drought stress; gall tissue versus leaf context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qRT-PCR correlation with gallic acid; subcellular localization; transient overexpression in tobacco; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gallic acid accumulation and tannin hydrolysis; increases GA and EGC in heterologous leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcTA1 is a tandem-duplicated tannase gene highly expressed in Galla chinensis and positively correlated with gallic acid content. 2. Tobacco transient expression increased gallic acid and epigallocatechin accumulation. 3. Recombinant RcTA1 hydrolyzed EGCG to GA and EGC and hydrolyzed PGG to GA, establishing RcTA1 as a central enzyme in tannin hydrolysis and GA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal phenolic; precursor and hydrolysis product associated with hydrolyzable tannins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Galla chinensis (galls); leaves at lower levels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states RcTA1 plays a central role in gallic acid accumulation, a precursor of hydrolyzable tannins.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering gallic acid and hydrolyzable tannin traits in medicinal plants.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSA: CRA012509; CRA012506; CRA012504; CRA012500); LC-MS/MS metabolomics; HPLC

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hydrolyzable tannin hydrolysis / gallic acid accumulation via RcTA1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates RcTA1 transcription and upregulates RcTA1/NbTA1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator promoting gallic acid and hydrolyzable tannin production through RcTA1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Rhus chinensis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in Galla chinensis relative to leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient overexpression in R. chinensis and tobacco leaves; tested in Arabidopsis protoplast reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Gall tissue developmental context; transient overexpression assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; dual-luciferase assay in tobacco leaves; luciferase assay in Arabidopsis protoplasts; transient overexpression in R. chinensis and tobacco; qRT-PCR and metabolite measurement
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases RcTA1 expression and gallic acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcDIV1 is an MYB TF highly expressed in Galla chinensis and associated with gallic acid/tannic acid content. 2. RcDIV1 activated the RcTA1 promoter in dual-luciferase and protoplast assays. 3. Transient overexpression upregulated RcTA1 (and NbTA1 in tobacco) and increased gallic acid accumulation, indicating promotion of hydrolyzable tannin hydrolysis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive phenolics with antioxidant and therapeutic value
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text contains an inconsistency: Y1H states RcDIV3 directly binds the RcTA1 promoter MBS, whereas functional activation assays support RcDIV1 as the activator; extraction follows the paper's main conclusion that RcDIV1 promotes RcTA1 transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target for boosting gallic acid and hydrolyzable tannin traits through transcriptional control of RcTA1.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSA: CRA012509; CRA012506; CRA012504; CRA012500); metabolite-transcript correlation network

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Homeobox-leucine zipper (HD-ZIP) transcription factor; GLABRA2 homolog
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation, likely activation of trichome development program
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Glandular trichome development; trichome-mediated ion secretion and stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly upregulates antioxidant-related genes AtPER2; AtSODC1; AtCATA1; AtAPX1 in Arabidopsis overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central regulator of glandular trichome development enabling secretion/deposition of salt-like metabolites and ions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts downstream of conserved MBW trichome regulatory module by homology/inference
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young fruits and pericarps; enriched in trichome-rich tissues; higher protein abundance in fruits than leaves
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental trichome program; salt stress context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; immunohistochemistry; western blot; Arabidopsis overexpression; salt tolerance assays; Na+ flux measurement by NMT; ROS and antioxidant enzyme assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes trichome formation and enhances salt tolerance with increased trichome-mediated Na+ efflux
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcGL2 is a GL2 homolog highly expressed in fruit pericarp glandular trichomes. 2. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased trichome number and development. 3. RcGL2-overexpressing lines showed greater salt tolerance, higher trichome Na+ efflux, lower ROS accumulation, and higher antioxidant enzyme activities.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salt-like secretions including phenolic acids and organic acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to salinity adaptation and surface deposition on fruits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a direct metabolic enzyme, RcGL2 is a core regulator connecting trichome development to metabolite/ion secretion and salinity adaptation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for engineering epidermal secretion traits, trichome density, and salt tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GSA: CRA012509; CRA012506; CRA012504; CRA012500); LC-MS/MS of secretions; ionomics
